region_of_origin: Global (major producers: Canada, Kazakhstan, Russia; origin: Fertile Crescent ~8,000 BCE)
food_subgroup: Milled/ground flaxseeds (Linum usitatissimum). CRITICAL: only ground form provides adequate ALA bioavailability (~90%); whole seeds yield only ~10–15% ALA bioavailability as they pass through the GI tract largely undigested.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a flaxseed entry (FOODON_03315174) but no specific entry for the ground/milled form. Closest parent applied for whole flaxseed; ground form is a processing variant not separately coded in FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FLS applied as closest parent for flaxseed; specific milled/ground sub-facet not available in standard FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for ground/milled flaxseed as a food product. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical clinical serving of ground flaxseeds is 2 tablespoons (approximately 15g), providing ~80 kcal, ~2.7g protein, ~6.3g fat, ~3.4g ALA omega-3, and ~4.1g dietary fibre.

nutrient_source.title: Flaxseeds, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #12220
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC #12220 reports nutrient values for raw flaxseeds. Ground/milled flaxseeds have essentially identical macronutrient composition per 100g as the milling process does not alter nutrient content, only particle size and bioavailability. Values applied directly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Flaxseed — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~32 for flaxseed sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. The low GI is attributable to the high dietary fibre content (27.3g/100g), mucilage gel formation, and high fat content which collectively attenuate postprandial glucose response. GL per 15g serving calculated as: (32 × 28.9g available carbohydrate per 100g × 15g serving / 100) / 100 ≈ 1.4; input record states GL ~2.1 per serving which is consistent with negligible GL classification. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (30mg/100g); clinically demonstrated blood pressure reduction from ALA omega-3 and SDG lignans
hypertension.guidance: Ground flaxseed is highly suitable for hypertensive patients: sodium content is negligible at 30mg/100g, and a landmark RCT (Rodriguez-Leyva et al., 2013, Hypertension) demonstrated that 30g/day of ground flaxseed over 6 months reduced systolic blood pressure by ~10 mmHg and diastolic blood pressure by ~7 mmHg in hypertensive patients — one of the most clinically significant dietary blood pressure reductions documented for a single food.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (32), negligible glycemic load (~2 per 15g serving), high soluble fibre mucilage, high magnesium content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ground flaxseed is excellent for Type 2 Diabetes management: GI of 32 and GL of ~2 per clinical serving are negligible; the mucilage gel formed by soluble fibre slows gastric emptying and reduces postprandial glucose from co-consumed foods; high magnesium content (~392mg/100g; ~59mg per 2 tbsp serving) addresses the magnesium deficiency common in T2DM and improves insulin receptor signalling; ALA omega-3 may modestly improve insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Highest plant-source ALA omega-3 (~22.8g/100g), SDG lignans (~375mg/100g), LDL-lowering soluble fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ground flaxseed is one of the most cardioprotective foods available: it provides ~22.8g ALA omega-3 per 100g (the richest plant-based ALA source), SDG lignans at ~375mg/100g (approximately 100× the lignan content of sesame), and soluble fibre that reduces LDL cholesterol by approximately 8–10%; the combination of ALA, lignans, and fibre supports endothelial function, reduces inflammation, and lowers cardiovascular risk; cholesterol content is confirmed zero.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High ALA omega-3 (~3.4g per 2 tbsp serving), SDG lignans with anti-inflammatory properties, high magnesium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ground flaxseed provides ~3.4g ALA omega-3 per standard 2 tbsp serving — the highest ALA content of any practical food serving — along with SDG lignans with mild anti-inflammatory properties and magnesium (~59mg per 2 tbsp) supporting anti-inflammatory enzyme function, making it a beneficial addition to the diet of rheumatoid arthritis patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ground flaxseeds (milled linseeds) are the mechanically milled form of Linum usitatissimum seeds, one of the world's oldest cultivated crops originating from the Fertile Crescent (~8,000 BCE). Per 100g, ground flaxseeds provide approximately 534 kcal, 18.3g protein, 42.2g fat (of which ~22.8g is ALA omega-3, the highest plant-based ALA content of any common food), 28.9g carbohydrate, and an exceptional 27.3g dietary fibre. Sodium is negligible at 30mg/100g. The standard clinical serving is 2 tablespoons (~15g), providing ~80 kcal and ~3.4g ALA omega-3. CRITICAL BIOAVAILABILITY NOTE: whole flaxseeds must NOT be substituted — whole seeds pass through the gastrointestinal tract largely undigested, yielding only ~10–15% ALA bioavailability, whereas ground/milled seeds provide ~90% bioavailability. Ground flaxseeds have a mild, nutty flavour and fine powdery texture that blends easily into smoothies, oatmeal, yogurt, and baked goods. They are a functional food staple in Singapore's health food market (available at Cold Storage, NTUC FairPrice, Guardian Pharmacy) since approximately 2010. Key clinical evidence: (1) GI ~32 (low), GL ~2 per serving (negligible) — excellent for glycaemic management; (2) SDG lignan content ~375mg/100g, approximately 100× that of sesame seeds — the richest dietary lignan source; (3) RCT evidence (Rodriguez-Leyva 2013, Hypertension): 30g/day reduced SBP by 10 mmHg and DBP by 7 mmHg over 6 months; (4) LDL cholesterol reduction of approximately 8–10% with regular consumption. Dietary flags: vegan, vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, dairy-free. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC #12220. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC #12220, an approved primary source with full macro and micronutrient panel. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated with verified data. Clinical evidence for health context is supported by peer-reviewed RCT literature.
notes: Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC #12220 — flaxseeds are a plant food and contain no dietary cholesterol. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero per USDA FDC #12220. Vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived components. Vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA FDC #12220. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FDC #12220. Monounsaturated_fat_g (7.53g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (28.73g) values derived from USDA FDC #12220 fatty acid breakdown: total fat 42.2g comprises saturated 3.7g + monounsaturated 7.53g + polyunsaturated 28.73g (of which ALA ~22.8g). Water_g (6.96g) sourced from USDA FDC #12220. BIOAVAILABILITY CRITICAL NOTE: This record applies to ground/milled flaxseeds only. Whole flaxseeds (FDC #12220 base entry) have identical per-100g nutrient composition but only ~10–15% ALA bioavailability due to intact seed coat preventing lipase access; grinding increases ALA bioavailability to ~90%. The 2 tbsp (~15g) clinical serving is the standard dose used in cardiovascular and glycaemic RCTs. SDG lignan content (~375mg/100g) is not captured in standard USDA nutrient fields; this value is from published literature (Adolphe et al., 2010; Thompson et al., 1997) and is noted for LLM context only, not in per_100g fields. FoodEx2 code A0FLS and FoodOn URI FOODON_03315174 are applied as closest-parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for the ground/milled form specifically.
